title: SELinux And Default File Permissions
description: Practice RHCSA v9 SELinux troubleshooting together with default permissions, SELinux port labels, and booleans.

tasks: Set the system-wide default umask to 027 for interactive shells.
Update Apache so /srv/rhcsa/selinux-site/index.html is served at http://localhost:8089/ while SELinux remains enforcing.
Apply persistent SELinux file-context and port-label changes so the site path and TCP port 8089 continue to work after relabel or reboot.
Set the SELinux boolean httpd_can_network_connect to on persistently.
Keep SELinux in enforcing mode throughout the task.
hints: A profile.d script is acceptable for the umask task.
Use semanage fcontext and restorecon for persistent labeling.
Apache must be pointed to the custom content directory before curl will work.
Use semanage port for the custom TCP port and getsebool or setsebool for the boolean.
solution_outline: Create a profile.d file that sets umask 027.
Update Apache to use the custom directory and listen on TCP port 8089.
Persistently label the custom site directory with the correct httpd content type and assign port 8089 to http_port_t.
Set the required SELinux boolean persistently and confirm the page works while SELinux stays enforcing.

tasks: Set the default shell umask to 027 system-wide.
Serve /srv/rhcsa/selinux-site/index.html with Apache on TCP port 8089 while SELinux remains enforcing.
Apply persistent SELinux labeling and port labeling so the content continues to work.
Set the SELinux boolean httpd_can_network_connect persistently to on.
